description: Network-based gaming console detection with LED color feedback on ESP32

setup: Install Arduino CLI: brew install arduino-cli
Install ESP32 board: arduino-cli core install esp32:esp32
Install libraries: arduino-cli lib install FastLED ESPping ESPmDNS
deploy: Connect ESP32 via USB to /dev/ttyUSB0 or /dev/cu.*

sync_instructions.description: Guidelines for syncing features between Python (main) and ESP32 branches
principles: Each branch maintains independent implementation appropriate for its platform
Feature parity is goal, not exact code parity
Manifest updates should be mirrored to both branches
Detection logic (MAC_OUI, STATIC_LIST, PORT_PROBE, MDNS) should be consistent
when_to_sync: Adding new supported brands and colors
Fixing bugs that apply to both platforms
UI/UX improvements to web config interface

sync_process.step2: Test thoroughly on that platform (compile + verify)
sync_process.step3: Port logic to other branch (adapt code, not copy-paste)
sync_process.step5: Update both manifests to reflect new feature
sync_process.step6: Commit with descriptive message mentioning both branches

do_not_sync: Language-specific patterns (Arduino loops vs Python threads)

components.esp32.ino: Main sketch - non-blocking loop, WiFi reconnect, web server
components.config.h: Hardware config, WiFi credentials, colors, OUI prefixes
components.network_scanner.h: Static device validator (no ARP sweep on ESP32)
components.web_ui.h: HTML/JS UI with edit/delete device management

architecture.flow: WiFi connect -> Web server starts -> Loop: check devices -> ping each -> determine winner -> set LED

detection.device_management: Web UI with add/edit/delete via /api/device endpoints

endpoints: POST /save - Add or update device (requires ip, brand, edit_mode, edit_idx)
GET /api/devices - JSON list of all devices
GET /api/device?idx=N - JSON for single device (edit mode)

firmware_features.non_blocking_loop: millis() based timing - web server always responsive
